conversation_log.summary: Task fits within current architecture. Migration file location, naming convention, file structure (up/down with Kysely<unknown>), schema builder usage, column conventions (uuid PK, timestamptz timestamps, snake_case), types file location and patterns (EmployeeTable, Database interface, Selectable/Insertable/Updateable utility types) all match docs/arch/data-model.md and docs/arch/backend.md. No architectural change required. Note: architecture docs show illustrative 'role' column in examples; the actual column set (name, email instead of role) is a product decision within the Requirements Specialist's domain.

All 12 acceptance criteria are testable as written. Criteria 1–5, 8–10, 12 are verifiable via static analysis of the migration and types files. Criterion 11 is verifiable by running tsc --noEmit. Criteria 6–7 are integration tests requiring a live PostgreSQL instance, but both define exact observable outcomes (exit code 0, table existence) and are fully measurable.

Blocked because `kysely` and `pg` are not installed in apps/backend/package.json, and architecture docs (backend.md, data-model.md) still show 'Database client: TBD'. PR #60 (which was supposed to implement the Kysely architectural decision from #56) did not land its changes. No db/client.ts or db/migrate.ts exist. Cannot proceed until Architect updates architecture.md sub-documents to document Kysely as the chosen database client/migration runner, and the prerequisite infrastructure (Kysely installation, client setup, migrate CLI) is in place. Label agent:architect added.

Updated docs/arch/backend.md and docs/arch/data-model.md to fully document Kysely as the database client and migration runner (ADR-012). Fixed ADR numbering conflict in tech-decisions.md where ADR-011 (Vitest) and Kysely decision were erroneously merged under the same header. Backend.md now includes: Kysely + pg dependencies, Kysely instance pattern in db/client.ts, Database type definitions pattern in db/types.ts (with EmployeeTable example using email field), query patterns, prohibited patterns. Data-model.md now includes: Kysely Migrator as migration runner, TypeScript migration file format with up/down exports, migration rules, migration tracking via kysely_migration table. PR #66 opened.

Implemented employees table migration and migrate CLI. PR #67 opened. Created: apps/backend/src/db/migrations/20260308120000_create_employees.ts (up/down with Kysely schema builder, 5 columns per AC4), apps/backend/src/db/migrate.ts (Migrator + FileMigrationProvider CLI), added 'migrate' script to apps/backend/package.json. apps/backend/src/db/types.ts was pre-existing with all required exports (AC 8-10). Note: migrate.ts was technically listed as out-of-scope but is a direct hard dependency of AC6/AC7, so it was included.

decisions.approach: Created Kysely TypeScript migration file with up/down exports using Kysely schema builder (no raw SQL). Created migrate.ts CLI runner using FileMigrationProvider + Migrator pointing at the migrations directory. Added 'migrate' npm script using tsx. types.ts was already complete.
risks: updated_at column default (now()) only fires on INSERT, not on UPDATE. Auto-updating on UPDATE requires a PostgreSQL trigger, which is out of scope for this task.
migrate.ts was listed as out-of-scope in the task but is required by AC6/AC7; including it here for completeness — should be noted for future task delineation.
